statusLine: EU fuel security remains under strain with 21 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, though overall stockpiles hover near mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security shows concerning fragility across multiple member states, with 21 of 27 countries maintaining critical stock levels in at least one fuel category. While EU-wide averages for petrol (98.9 days) and diesel (98 days) remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum, jet fuel stocks average just 91.9 days - barely meeting requirements. This narrow margin leaves little buffer for supply disruptions.

Eastern European nations face the most severe shortages, with Romania displaying critical levels across all three fuel types and Poland maintaining dangerously low reserves. Major economies Germany, France, and Italy also show critical stockpiles in multiple categories, indicating widespread pressure on European fuel infrastructure. The geographic spread of these shortages - from Romania to Spain to Germany - suggests systemic rather than localized supply challenges.

Fuel pricing reflects underlying market tensions, with EU average petrol at €1.784/L and diesel at €2.021/L. The €0.976/L price spread between the Netherlands (€2.316) and Malta (€1.34) for petrol reveals significant market fragmentation. Brent crude at $94.64/barrel, despite a recent $0.84 decline, remains elevated following the 27% increase since Middle East conflicts began, contributing to sustained pressure on European fuel markets.
keyPoints: 21 of 27 EU countries maintain critical stock levels in at least one fuel type, with Romania, Poland, and Luxembourg showing critical reserves across all categories
Jet fuel stocks average just 91.9 days EU-wide - barely above the 90-day minimum requirement and indicating vulnerability in aviation fuel supply
Major economies including Germany, France, and Italy display critical stockpiles in multiple fuel categories, suggesting widespread infrastructure pressure
Fuel prices show significant fragmentation with a €0.976/L spread in petrol costs between Netherlands and Malta, reflecting uneven market conditions
Brent crude remains elevated at $94.64/barrel despite recent declines, maintaining pressure from the 27% increase following Middle East supply disruptions
